REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (722 - 19th Avenue, Sf Ca)
Off Calendar Aug-02-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Shustoff, for and with petitioner Elizabeth Adler; Tim Curran; Peter Jacobs.

The Court noted that there was no proof of publication for the amended report of sale in the Court's file, and inquired as to whether such publication was needed prior to the sale. Mr. Shustoff replied that he had been advised by the Probate Examiner that no publication of the amended report was required. The Court stated that he would proceed with the sale, but that the order confirming sale would be conditioned upon satisfaction that the notice requirements had been satisfied.

Mr. Shustoff stated that he was presenting the petiton to confirm sale of a 100% interest in the real property located at 722 19th Avenue, San Francisco, California, upn the terms of the amended report of sale filed July 14, 2016. The offering bid was $940,000.00. The Court stated that this was a qualifying offer, and asked if there wewre any overbids. An auction then took place, with the highest bid of $1,028,000.00 placed by Tim Curran as agent for buyers Belen Lupera and Henri Revel.

The sale was confirmed at $1,028,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the amended report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order and compliance with notice requirements). No additional bond was required.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not reported)

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (722 - 19th Avenue, Sf Ca)
Off Calendar Jul-12-2016 Continued To Aug-02-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Shustoff for (petitioner) Elizabeth Adler who is present in court.

The Court stated that there is a question regarding bond, that it was ordered to be increased to $1,537,000.00 on 7/7/16 and asked if bond has been filed. Mr. Shustoff stated that it has not and that they were waiting for today's hearing as the amount will need to be adjusted. The Court stated that once bond is ordered the court expects it to be promptly taken care of. The court proceeded with the sale based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed on 5/23/16. Tim Curran of Coldwell Realty was present to overbid on the property but stated that he would need 30-35 days to close instead of the 20-21 days as listed in the petition. The Court stated that the terms are what they are. Mr. Shustoff stated that the original buyers have asked to increase the closing date to September 1, 2016. The Court asked if the change in terms was filed with the court, that it would need to be, that there may be other bidders interested if they know that they had additional time and that it will require an amended report of sale. Mr. Shustoff stated that they will also have to re-notice all parties. The Report of Sale and Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property was continued to August 2, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (722 - 19th Avenue, Sf Ca)
Off Calendar Jun-21-2016 Continued To Jul-12-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Shustoff for petitioner Elizabeth Adler.

Mr. Shustoff requested a continuance of the hearing to July 12, 2016, so that he could give proper notice and file an ex parte application to increase the bond. As requested, the matter was continued to July 12, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: John Boncato. (Not reported)
